nodes.label: README.md (Spring AI MCP — Org Enterprise Assistant)
llm-mcp-client (MCP Client / Enterprise AI Assistant)
nodes.rationale: Has no datasource — proxies user messages to OpenAI and dispatches tool calls to downstream MCP servers over Streamable HTTP

nodes.label: mcp-server-hr-service (HR Service :8084)
nodes.rationale: Manages employee leave and replacement lookups

nodes.label: mcp-server-ticket-service (Ticket Service :8081)
nodes.rationale: Manages support tickets; exposes analyze-tickets MCP prompt

nodes.label: mcp-server-deployment-service (Deployment Service :8082)
nodes.rationale: Manages deployment scheduling; uses STREAMABLE MCP protocol while other servers use STATELESS

nodes.label: mcp-server-notification-service (Notification Service :8083)
nodes.rationale: Sends and lists notifications across channels (INTERNAL, EMAIL, SLACK)

nodes.label: mcp-inspector container (docker-compose)
nodes.rationale: Runs @modelcontextprotocol/inspector via npx for local MCP debugging; auth proxy token disabled for dev convenience (DANGEROUSLY_OMIT_AUTH=true)

nodes.label: llm-mcp-client Insomnia Collection
nodes.rationale: Requests against the llm-mcp-client (Enterprise AI Assistant) — the MCP client orchestrating HR, Ticket, Deployment, Notification, GitHub, and Gmail MCP servers via natural-language chat

nodes.label: mcp-travel-service (container)
nodes.rationale: Resource limits (0.5 CPU / 600M memory) and JAVA_OPTS heap sizing chosen to keep the containerized MCP server lightweight; healthcheck polls actuator/health for orchestrator readiness.

nodes.label: mcp-travel-service
nodes.rationale: Flight availability MCP server using the Amadeus API; runs in STATELESS MCP server protocol mode and exposes graceful shutdown with a 30s shutdown-phase timeout.

nodes.label: Grafana Dashboards Provisioning
nodes.rationale: Auto-loads dashboard JSON from a provider directory into an 'Org MCP' folder so dashboards appear in Grafana without manual import.

nodes.label: Grafana Datasources Provisioning
nodes.rationale: Wires Prometheus as default metrics datasource and Tempo as tracing datasource, with exemplar trace-ID linking and service-map/node-graph integration so metrics and traces correlate in Grafana.

nodes.label: prometheus.yml (scrape config)
nodes.rationale: Apps run on the host via ./mvnw spring-boot:run while Prometheus runs in Docker, so scrape targets use host.docker.internal to reach each service's /actuator/prometheus endpoint.

nodes.label: tempo.yml (Tempo config)
nodes.rationale: Configures OTLP receivers (HTTP 4318 / gRPC 4317), local trace storage backend, and query-frontend SLOs for trace search/lookup.

hyperedges.label: Shared PostgreSQL Instance with Per-Service Flyway Isolation
Observability Stack: Prometheus + Tempo + Grafana wired across MCP services
Shared MCP token-auth/rate-limit security pattern across services
